LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. PLAINTIFF 1095 MARKET STREET HOTEL, LLC DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE THAT IT IS AN INTENDED THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE CLEVELAND/PLANT SUBCONTRACT. THE INTENT TO BENEFIT A THIRD PARTY MUST APPEAR "ON THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT." WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-09-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT - CROSS-DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IN THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT CROSS-COMPLAINANT 1095 MARKET STREET HOTEL LLC IS OVERRULED. SEE MINUTES FOR DETAILS. 1095 IS REQUIRED TO FILE A COMPLETE COPY OF THE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT BY JUNE 5, 2017 AND TITLE IT "SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT." THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT SHALL INCLUDE THE MISSING PAGES AND EXHIBITS THAT THE PLEADING REFERENCES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: IRENE TOFFT, CSR # 12313

Off Calendar May-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Off calendar. A notice of settlement of the entire case was filed May 4, 2018. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2017 Set For Trial On Jun-25-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SARAH A. MARSEY OF THE HAIGHT BROWN & BONESTEEL LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-546-7500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT 1095 MARKET STREET HOTEL;
ATTY. TED R. GROPMAN OF PEPPER HAMILTON LLP, TEL. NO. 1-213-928-9850 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT - Cross-defendant Cleveland Wrecking Company's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract in the first amended cross-complaint cross-complainant 1095 Market Street Hotel LLC is overruled. 1095 correctly cites CACI 301 for the proposition that determination of whether 1095 is a third party beneficiary requires consideration of all of the circumstances that existed when the subcontract was executed. The text of the subcontract indicates that the instrument was to benefit the owner as well as the general contractor. For example, paragraph 12 provides that "[s]ubcontractor shall at all times indemnify and save Contractor and Owner harmless against all liability for claims and liens for labor." Cleveland raises a factual issue regarding whether 1095 was in fact the owner. 1095's allegations at paragraph 49 of the first amended cross-complaint that it was the owner must be accepted as true for purposes of this demurrer. Cleveland's argument that it did not even know about 1095 fails. That is an evidentiary issue not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Moreover, the intent of the promisee (Plant), not the promissor (Cleveland) is what controls. (Souza v. Westlands Water Dist. (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 879, 893.) 1095 is required to file a complete copy of the amended cross-complaint by June 5, 2017 and title it "Second Amended Cross-Complaint." The Second Amended Complaint shall include the missing pages and exhibits that the pleading references. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-15-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ted R. Gropman of the law offices of Pepper Hamilton LLP (213.928.9800) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/demurring party, Cleveland Wrecking

Attorney, Sarah A. Marsey of the law offices of Haight Brown & Bonesteel LLP (415.546.7500) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, 1095 Market Street Hotel LLC

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS-DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract and OVERRULED as to the fifth cause of action for negligence. Plaintiff 1095 Market Street Hotel, LLC does not sufficiently allege that it is an intended third party beneficiary of the Cleveland/Plant subcontract. The intent to benefit a third party must appear "on the terms of the contract." (Bancomer, S.A. v. Superior Court (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1450, 1458.) Paragraph 44 of the complaint alleges that "[t]he Cleveland Contract was entered into by defendants expressly to benefit Plaintiff." The face of the subcontract and Addendum F to that subcontract do not reflect an intent to benefit 1095 Market, however. 1095 Market is not named in the subcontract and the owner of the project is identified as "Oliver and Associates." 1095 Market is given leave to plead facts to support its assertion that it is an intended third party beneficiary of the subcontract. In paragraphs 15 and 16 of its complaint, 1095 Market alleges sufficient facts regarding property damage to support its nuisance claim. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
